title: Unify medium blue-green gradient background and enforce dark text/icon contrast across Busy AI

requirements.summary: Unify all major shells/screens and routes under a consistent medium-intensity blue-green gradient background with no near-white or purple-heavy washout.
acceptanceCriteria: All major shells/screens (AppShell layout, LoginPage, AuthGate initializing/redirect views) visibly share the same gradient background treatment.
The background is a medium-intensity blue-green gradient (not white/blank-looking, not purple-heavy).
No screen renders as a plain white/near-white background by default.

file_operations.description: Update the global background styling so the default document/body background and the shared .app-gradient-bg utility use the same medium-intensity blue-green gradient (avoid near-white stops and remove any purple bias), ensuring a consistent non-white baseline even before route-level layout renders.

Ensure AppShell’s outer wrapper remains the single source of truth for applying the unified .app-gradient-bg background across all authenticated routes, and remove/avoid any competing background classes that could cause washout.
Align AuthGate initializing and redirect screens to the same unified .app-gradient-bg background and ensure no additional overlay/background causes the screen to appear plain white/near-white.
Align LoginPage with the unified .app-gradient-bg background and adjust/remove any extra background overlay that could visually lighten the page into a near-white/blank-looking state.

requirements.summary: Make all user-visible text default to very dark tones (not white) while maintaining readability over the medium blue-green gradient across all pages.
acceptanceCriteria: No primary UI text relies on white text for readability (e.g., remove hardcoded "text-white" usage in app content).
Typography remains clearly readable on every page (Chat, Maker, Analytics, Gallery, Shop, Login) without appearing washed out.
Muted/supporting text remains readable (still dark enough to be visible).

file_operations.description: Adjust theme tokens (e.g., --foreground, --muted-foreground, and *-foreground tokens used by buttons/badges) so default typography renders in very dark tones and supporting text remains dark enough over the new medium gradient; keep prefers-reduced-motion behavior intact for existing animations.
Remove reliance on light/white button text (e.g., text-primary-foreground on gradient buttons) by switching to dark text styling that remains readable on the updated medium blue-green theme.

Update any remaining button and emphasis text styling that depends on light/white foreground tokens (e.g., btn-glow usage with text-primary-foreground) so all visible text defaults to very dark and remains readable.
Update feature card and CTA text styling to avoid white text and ensure headings, descriptions, and button labels use dark tones that stay readable over the medium gradient.
Update any remaining highlighted/CTA areas that use white text by default (e.g., btn-glow button text) so all user-visible text uses dark tones while preserving clarity.

requirements.summary: Standardize icons across the app to use the same dark color style as text (no white icons), including navigation, loaders, chat, and gallery icons.
acceptanceCriteria: Sidebar icons and labels appear in a consistent dark tone in both active and inactive states (inactive can be slightly lighter but still dark/visible).
AuthGate loader/spinner and any lucide icons render in dark tones (no white icons).
Chat UI does not render assistant/user icons in forced white; icons follow the dark icon rule.
Gallery item icons follow the dark icon rule and remain visible against their containers.

file_operations.description: Change the AuthGate loader/spinner and any related iconography to render in a dark tone (e.g., use foreground-based icon coloring) rather than lighter/white styling.
Ensure lucide-react sidebar icons inherit the same dark tone as labels in both active/inactive states, avoiding any implicit white icon styling and keeping inactive still dark/visible.
Ensure header/menu icons (e.g., the mobile Menu icon button) render with dark icon styling consistent with the app’s text color (no white icons).
Remove forced white chat icons (e.g., Bot icon with text-white) and ensure both assistant/user iconography uses dark tones consistent with the updated text color rules.
Update gallery card icon styling (type icon, view/download icons) so icons render in dark tones and remain visible against their containers without using white.
Update maker feature icons that are currently forced to white so they adopt the app’s dark icon style while maintaining visibility on their background containers.
Update any remaining forced white icons (e.g., the coming-soon hero icon) to use the same dark icon style as text and remain visible on the updated theme surfaces.

requirements.summary: Remove remaining purple/blue gradient styling and overly-dark/black surfaces that conflict with the new medium blue-green theme while keeping cards/panels readable and distinct.
acceptanceCriteria: Chat bubbles/avatars do not use blue-to-purple gradients or slate-950/near-black panels that reduce theme consistency.
Gallery cards do not use near-black backgrounds that fight the global medium gradient; cards remain clearly separated from the background with borders/shadows/tint.
The app maintains a consistent blue-green visual language across pages (no purple-heavy accents remaining in core UI surfaces).

file_operations.description: Replace blue-to-purple gradient chat bubbles and near-black assistant panels with blue-green-aligned, lighter tinted surfaces that keep dark text readable and maintain consistent theme language.

Remove purple-heavy/near-black styling (e.g., dark input background and blue-to-purple send button gradient) and restyle input/button surfaces to match the medium blue-green theme with dark text/icons.

Replace the near-black gallery card background (e.g., bg-slate-950/50) and purple-heavy/pink/orange gradient accents with blue-green-consistent surfaces, borders, and subtle tints/shadows that keep the card readable and separated from the global gradient.
Update maker feature accent gradients and icon containers to remove purple-heavy accents (e.g., pink-to-purple) and align highlights with the blue-green visual language while keeping cards distinct.

Update shared gradient utilities (e.g., .btn-glow and any reusable gradient helpers) to avoid purple-heavy accents and support the medium blue-green theme without introducing overly-dark/black surfaces.
